steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The study measured two things across 17 languages: syllable rate (varied widely) and information rate (stayed constant at ~39 bits per second). The correct choice must capture both findings — that one metric differed while the other remained the same.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researchers found that information was conveyed more quickly in Spanish, at 7.7 syllables per second, than in Vietnamese, at 5.3 syllables per second.: misses the key contrast between the two metrics. ✗
  • Choice B — The 2021 study determined the information rate (IR) of 17 languages in bits of information conveyed per second.: does not capture that information rate stayed constant across all languages. ✗
  • Choice C — Though some of the languages differed in number of syllables spoken per second, all 17 conveyed information at roughly the same rate.: accurately captures both findings — variation in syllable rate alongside a constant information rate. ✓
  • Choice D — Vietnamese had the sixteenth fastest syllable rate, lower than that of Spanish, which had the second-fastest; however Spanish had the lower information rate of the two.: contradicts the study's finding that information rate was approximately equal across all 17 languages. ✗
That is the correct answer.
